meta.subject: Mathematics
notation: Use the 'therefore' symbol (∴) before final answers
Use decimal point (not comma) for decimals
Coordinates written as (x; y) with semicolon
markingStandards: Full marks require all working shown
meta.assessmentFormat: Paper 1: Numbers, Operations and Relationships; Patterns, Functions and Algebra. Paper 2: Space and Shape (Geometry), Measurement, Data Handling (including Probability).

subtopics.name: Whole Numbers
skills: Describe the real number system by recognising, defining and distinguishing properties of natural numbers, whole numbers, integers, rational numbers and irrational numbers
Use prime factorisation of numbers to find LCM and HCF
Solve problems in contexts involving whole numbers, ratio, rate and proportion
methods: Prime factorisation for finding LCM and HCF
commonSteps: Identify the type of number and its properties

subtopics.name: Integers
skills: Perform calculations involving all four operations with integers
Perform calculations involving squares, cubes, square roots and cube roots of integers
methods: Sign rules for operations with integers

subtopics.name: Common Fractions, Decimal Fractions and Percentages
skills: Add, subtract, multiply and divide common fractions and mixed numbers
Convert between common fractions, decimal fractions and percentages
Calculate squares, cubes, square roots and cube roots of fractions and decimals
Solve problems involving percentage increase, decrease and profit/loss
methods: LCD for addition/subtraction; reciprocal for division
notation: Mixed numbers or improper fractions — follow question instruction

subtopics.name: Exponents
skills: Compare and represent numbers in exponential form and scientific notation (including negative exponents)
Apply the general laws of exponents: aᵐ × aⁿ = aᵐ⁺ⁿ, aᵐ ÷ aⁿ = aᵐ⁻ⁿ (m > n), (aᵐ)ⁿ = aᵐⁿ, (a × b)ⁿ = aⁿ × bⁿ, a⁰ = 1, a⁻ⁿ = 1/aⁿ
Perform calculations using numbers in exponential form
Solve problems involving exponents
methods: Scientific notation for very large or small numbers

subtopics.name: Numeric and Geometric Patterns; Functions and Relationships
skills: Investigate and extend numeric and geometric patterns (including non-constant difference/ratio patterns)
Describe and justify general rules in words or algebraic language
Determine input/output values or rules using flow diagrams, tables, formulae and equations
Determine, interpret and justify equivalence of different descriptions of the same relationship (verbal, diagrams, tables, formulae, equations, graphs)

subtopics.name: Algebraic Expressions
skills: Recognise conventions for writing algebraic expressions
Identify and classify like and unlike terms, coefficients and exponents
Recognise monomials, binomials and trinomials
Expand and simplify algebraic expressions (including products of binomials and trinomials)
Factorise algebraic expressions (common factor, difference of squares, trinomials)
methods: Grouping and common factors for factorisation
keyFormulae: (a + b)(a + c) = a² + a(c + b) + bc

subtopics.name: Algebraic Equations
skills: Solve linear equations (including those with brackets and variables on both sides)
Solve equations by factorisation (where one side is factorised to zero)
Set up and solve equations from word problems
methods: Factorise then apply zero product rule

subtopics.name: Geometry of Straight Lines and 2D Shapes
skills: Revise and apply angle relationships (straight line, at a point, vertically opposite, corresponding, alternate, co-interior)
Classify 2D shapes (triangles and quadrilaterals) by sides and angles
Investigate properties of triangles using constructions (including exterior angle theorem)
Solve problems using properties of 2D shapes
methods: Triangle angle sum and exterior angles

subtopics.name: Theorem of Pythagoras
skills: Use Pythagoras theorem to solve problems in 2D (including unknown sides and converse)
Apply in context (e.g., distances, heights)
methods: a² + b² = c² where c is hypotenuse
notation: Leave in surd form unless instructed

subtopics.name: Constructions
skills: Perform basic geometric constructions (bisectors, perpendiculars, triangles from given data)
Use constructions to investigate properties of figures

subtopics.name: Area and Perimeter of 2D Shapes
skills: Calculate perimeter and area of polygons and circles using formulae and SI unit conversions
Solve problems involving perimeter and area
Investigate effect of doubling dimensions on perimeter and area
methods: Break composite shapes into basic figures
commonSteps: Include units and round as required
keyFormulae: Rectangle: A = l × b, P = 2(l + b)

subtopics.name: Surface Area and Volume of 3D Objects
skills: Calculate surface area and volume of prisms and cylinders
Classify 3D objects (including Platonic solids: properties of faces, vertices, edges)
Solve problems involving surface area and volume
methods: Surface area = sum of all faces
keyFormulae: Rectangular prism: V = l × b × h, SA = 2(lb + lh + bh)

subtopics.name: Collecting, Organising, Summarising and Representing Data
skills: Pose questions on social, economic and environmental issues
Select sources, distinguish samples and populations, choose data collection methods
Organise and summarise data using measures of central tendency (mean, median, mode) and dispersion (range, extremes, outliers)
Draw and interpret bar graphs, histograms and other representations
Analyse and report on data

subtopics.name: Probability
skills: Determine probabilities for compound events using two-way tables and tree diagrams
Predict relative frequency from probability and compare with experimental results
Explain possible differences between relative frequency and theoretical probability
methods: Use tables and diagrams for compound events
